questions.question: What prefix must all custom data attributes use in HTML5?
questions.explanation: HTML5 custom data attributes must begin with 'data-' followed by at least one character, e.g., data-user-id="42".

questions.question: How do you access data-user-name in JavaScript using the dataset property?
questions.explanation: The dataset property converts hyphenated data attribute names to camelCase. So data-user-name becomes dataset.userName.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a valid data attribute name?
questions.explanation: Data attribute names must not contain uppercase letters. 'data-XML' is invalid because it contains uppercase characters.

questions.question: What type of value do data attributes store?
questions.explanation: Data attributes always store string values in HTML. If you need a number or boolean, you must convert the string value in JavaScript.

questions.question: In JavaScript, the ___ property on an element provides access to all its data-* attributes.
questions.explanation: The dataset property returns a DOMStringMap of all data-* attributes on an element, with names converted from hyphenated to camelCase.

questions.question: The data attribute data-item-count is accessed in JavaScript as dataset.___.
questions.explanation: Hyphenated data attribute names are converted to camelCase when accessed via the dataset property. data-item-count becomes itemCount.
